sentence: Subjective:

HPI: The patient is a 2-year resident of the city who has noticed a change in his breathing over time, describing himself as short of breath, which he attributes to catching his breath more frequently than usual.
He reports that this symptom has been ongoing and is not sure why it occurs.
He also mentions that his eyes water occasionally, which he attributes to allergies, and has a dog that also experiences similar symptoms.
He has used an inhaler prescribed by urgent care to alleviate his symptoms in the past.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of heavy drinking and occasional diarrhea, but denies any other significant medical conditions or allergies.
Review of systems: The patient denies any symptoms of nausea, vomiting, rash, lip or throat swelling, or other issues.
Current medications: Albuterol inhaler, as prescribed by urgent care.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's pulse and pulse oximetry are within normal limits.
The patient appears to be in no distress.
Physical exam: The patient does not have any rash, angioedema, or audible stridor.
There is faint expiratory wheezing bilaterally in all lung fields.
Test Results: The patient had a normal chest x-ray and pulmonary function test.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has newly diagnosed allergic asthma.
Problem list: Asthma; Allergies; Unknown.
Plan:
I prescribed albuterol inhaler to be used as needed for symptom relief.
I also prescribed Singulair 10mg to be taken once daily to decrease the occurrences of asthma symptoms.
I will proceed with allergy testing, starting with skin testing, to identify potential triggers.
I will schedule the patient for follow-up in one week to discuss the skin testing results and further management.
Follow-up: I will see the patient next week to schedule the skin testing.
